heading: Milk manufacturing marketing adjustment
label: 7255 Milk manufacturing marketing adjustment
notes: CodificationSection is comprised of . Subsec. (e) of repealed and enacted provisions set out as a note under .section 145 of Pub. L. 104–127section 145 of Pub. L. 104–127section 1446e–1 of this titlesection 1446e–1 of this title

children.text: $1.65 per hundredweight of milk for milk manufactured into butter and nonfat dry milk; and
$1.80 per hundredweight of milk for milk manufactured into cheese.

the amount by which the product price value of butter and nonfat dry milk manufactured from a hundred pounds of milk containing 3.5 pounds of butterfat and 8.7 pounds of milk solids not fat resulting from a State’s yield and product price formulas exceeds the class price for the milk used to produce those products; or

the amount by which the product price value of cheese manufactured from a hundred pounds of milk containing 3.5 pounds of butterfat and 8.7 pounds of milk solids not fat resulting from a State’s yield and product price formulas exceeds the class price for the milk used to produce cheese.

children.heading: Effect of violation
children.label: c Effect of violation
children.text: If the Secretary determines following a hearing that a State has in effect a manufacturing allowance that exceeds the manufacturing allowance authorized in subsection (a), the Secretary shall suspend purchases of cheddar cheese, butter, and nonfat dry milk produced in that State until such time as the State complies with such subsection.

children.heading: Effective date; implementation
children.label: d Effective date; implementation
children.text: This section (other than subsection (e) ) shall be effective during the period beginning on the first day of the first month beginning after , and ending on . During that period, the Secretary may exercise the authority provided to the Secretary under this section without regard to the issuance of regulations intended to carry out this section.1See Codification note below.1April 4, 1996December 31, 1999
